title: Narada
tagline: Every answer traces back to a source.
pitch: Ask Narada about any topic and it hands back a table of facts, and every single one links straight back to the page it came from. No blended summaries, no unsourced claims slipped in as if they were checked.
technicalOverview: It works in stages: search the web, pull out the facts, verify each one, build the table. Each stage can run on a different model depending on what's cheaper or better for that step, and nothing enters the table without a source attached to it.
deepDive: That source attachment is enforced at the data model level, not just in the UI, so every cell is a value permanently paired with its origin URL. The pipeline runs on a provider abstraction layer over a single LiteLLM integration, supporting major LLM providers and 3 search providers, all hot-swappable through env config.

title: Donna
pitch: Donna handles scheduling, reschedules meetings when plans change, and messages clients directly to keep them in the loop. Book a call, need to move it, cancel last minute: Donna manages that conversation so the business owner doesn't have to.
technicalOverview: Underneath, it's not one assistant, it's a small team of them. A central agent handles the business owner's side, and a separate agent spawns for each client conversation so nothing gets crossed between clients. Every action gets checked before it happens, so it never touches a calendar on a guess.
deepDive: That checking layer is a typed MCP tool layer sitting in front of every database mutation, backed by Redis for agent state, with a two-stage LLM firewall screening messages both ways so a prompt injection attempt or a hallucinated booking never reaches a calendar in the first place.

title: RiskWise
tagline: Ask about a route, get an honest risk score.
pitch: Give RiskWise a shipping route and it returns a risk score, a plain-language reason for it, and a safer alternative if things look bad. Built for the actual question a logistics team asks before committing to a route.
technicalOverview: Three agents split the job. One watches for what's actually happening on that route right now. One turns that into a score across separate risk factors. One suggests an alternative if the number is bad enough to need it.
deepDive: Those three agents, Athena, Hermes, and Apollo, pull from 3 live public sources in parallel, score risk across 4 independent factors, and fall back through live data, then database, then LLM synthesis, so the system never returns nothing even when an external API is down.
